free_text: The (He3,d), (d,p) and (d,n) reactions on 28Si*
The error bars on the data points include
the statistical and background subtraction errors.
Angle digitizing error
Data digitizing error
SD, LV
l75 mu-g/cm2 thick self-supporting natural silicon
target.
The incident 3He beam was collected in a
Faraday cup and integrate the mean current was about
1.2 mm thick silicon surface
barrier "dE" detector, and a 10 mm thick
lithium-drifted silicon "E" detector, cooled to
liquid nitrogen temperature. An another dE -E
telescope including a 300 mu-mm thick silicon surface
barrier detector and a 3 mm thick lithium-drifted
silicon detector registered the elastically scattered
3He particles.
The target thickness was estimated from the
energy loss of alpha-particles from a 212Po source,
with an accuracy of +- 10%.
digitized data from fig.4 of ZP,271,139,1974

the Louvain University isochronous
cyclotron (Cyclone).
self-supporting natural silicon target, of which
the thickness (1.49+- 0.08 mg/cm2) was determined by
measuring the energy loss of 5.47 MeV alpha-particles
from an 241Am  source.
The beam was collected at 6m from the
target in a Faraday cup protected against secondary
electrons by a high voltage ring electrode.
dE-E telescope consisting of two
silicon surface-barrier detectors, 1 and 2 mm thick,
respectively.
a silicon surface barrier detector was set at
a fixed angle (65. angle) with respect to the incident
beam as a second monitor.

self-supporting natural silicon target, of which
the thickness (1.49+- 0.08 mg/cm2) was determined by
measuring the energy loss of 5.47 MeV alpha-particles
from an 241Am  source.
The beam was collected at 6m from the
target in a Faraday cup protected against secondary
electrons by a high voltage ring electrode.
The neutrons were detected in a 2 cm thick,
20 cm diameter NE 102 plastic scintillator, coupled via
a lucite light pipe to a Philips 58 AVP photomultiplier
